name: add-adjustment-layer-effect-20260327-0358
idea: Add adjustment layer / effect layer clips to the timeline. Users can drag a continuous effect from the effect creator onto a visual track, creating an 'effect clip' that applies its effect to all clips on tracks below it for its duration. Key concepts: (1) New clip type 'effect-layer' — a clip on a visual track that has no media content, just an effect applied to everything beneath it. (2) Can be created by dragging from the effect creator panel or from a new 'Add Effect Layer' button. (3) The effect layer clip is resizable and movable like any other clip. (4) Rendering: the Remotion composition needs to identify effect-layer clips and wrap all lower-track content within that time range with the effect component. (5) Effect layers show in the timeline with a distinctive visual style (e.g. semi-transparent, different color) so users can tell them apart from media clips. (6) Effect layers can use any continuous effect (built-in or custom) with tuneable parameters. (7) Multiple effect layers can stack — they compose in track order (top affects everything below it).

overrides.note: User wants a draggable effect-layer button in the TimelineCanvas footer (next to the existing text tool drag circle). Same drag-to-drop pattern: drag it onto the timeline to create an effect-layer clip at that position. Then click to configure the effect in the properties panel.

notes.note: User wants a draggable effect-layer button in the TimelineCanvas footer (next to the existing text tool drag circle). Same drag-to-drop pattern: drag it onto the timeline to create an effect-layer clip at that position. Then click to configure the effect in the properties panel.

last_gate.rationale: The remaining FLAG-003 is now an execution-level detail, not a planning gap. The plan correctly decided that same-track overlap is forbidden; the critique points out that the enforcement mechanism isn't spelled out in Step 8. But the fix is straightforward — the executor needs to call resolveOverlaps() or check for occupied ranges when creating effect-layer clips, same as any well-written drop handler should. This is implementation detail that belongs in the code, not a plan revision. The score trajectory (6.0 → 2.0) shows strong convergence, FLAG-001 and FLAG-002 are resolved, and another iteration would risk churn on a point that the executor can handle with a one-line note.
last_gate.signals_assessment: Weighted score dropped from 6.0 to 2.0 across two iterations — strong convergence. Plan delta was 76.6% (substantial revision that addressed all three original flags). No recurring critiques. The sole remaining flag (FLAG-003 overlap enforcement) is a refinement of an already-resolved design decision, not a new concern. Preflight is clean across all checks. No debt subsystem overlaps or escalations.
warnings: Step 8 (effect-layer drop handler) MUST include explicit overlap resolution — either call resolveOverlaps() or reject the drop if the target range is occupied. The executor should not assume this happens automatically.
Verify that clip move/drag operations also enforce the no-overlap rule for effect-layer clips, not just the initial drop path.

settled_decisions.decision: Reuse existing `continuous` field on TimelineClip for the effect-layer's effect config.
settled_decisions.rationale: Settled in iteration 1, carried forward. Minimizes type surface and leverages existing parameter/intensity infrastructure.
Settled in iteration 1. No source media means no from/to; hold path is well-tested.

settled_decisions.decision: Rendering uses React composition within Remotion (wrapping lower tracks in effect components).
settled_decisions.rationale: Settled in iteration 1. Stays within existing Remotion patterns.

settled_decisions.decision: Same-track overlap of effect-layer clips is forbidden. Stacking requires different visual tracks.
settled_decisions.rationale: Settled in iteration 2 to resolve FLAG-003. Gives deterministic composition order via existing track z-order without introducing a secondary ordering mechanism.

settled_decisions.decision: Draggable effect-layer button lives in TimelineCanvas footer next to the text-tool drag circle.
settled_decisions.rationale: Settled in iteration 2 per user direction and FLAG-001 resolution. Follows the identical drag pattern already established.

settled_decisions.decision: OverlayEditor skips effect-layer clips — no move/crop handles shown.
settled_decisions.rationale: Settled in iteration 2 to resolve FLAG-002. Effect-layer clips have no spatial bounds.

last_gate.orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.
